heading: Home leave; leave for Chiefs of Missions; leave for crews of vessels
label: 6305 Home leave; leave for Chiefs of Missions; leave for crews of vessels
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large(a).5 U.S.C. 2062(f), .Oct. 30, 1951, ch. 631, § 203(f)65 Stat. 680, “(f)”, .Sept. 6, 1960Pub. Delegation of FunctionsFunctions of President under subsec. (a) of this section delegated to Office of Personnel Management, see section 1(2) of Ex. Ord. No. 11228, , 30 F.R. 7739, set out as a note under , The President.June 14, 1965section 301 of Title 3

Ex. Ord. No. 10471. Authorization To Grant Leaves of AbsenceEx. Ord. No. 10471, , 18 F.R. 4231, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 12292, , 46 F.R. 13967, provided:July 17, 1953Feb. 23, 19811. The heads of the several departments and agencies of the Government are hereby authorized and empowered, without the approval, ratification, or other action of the President, to exercise, with respect to personnel in their respective department or agency, the authority conferred upon the President by of the United States Code, to authorize leaves of absence in accordance with the said section 6305(b) to persons who receive compensation in accordance with section 401 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 ().section 6305(b) of title 522 U.S.C. 39612. This order shall be effective as of .July 5, 1953

The heads of the several departments and agencies of the Government are hereby authorized and empowered, without the approval, ratification, or other action of the President, to exercise, with respect to personnel in their respective department or agency, the authority conferred upon the President by of the United States Code, to authorize leaves of absence in accordance with the said section 6305(b) to persons who receive compensation in accordance with section 401 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 ().section 6305(b) of title 522 U.S.C. 39612. This order shall be effective as of .July 5, 1953

children.text: is for use in the United States, or if the employee’s place of residence is outside the area of employment, in its territories or possessions including the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico;
accumulates for future use without regard to the limitation in ; andsection 6304(b) of this title
may not be made the basis for terminal leave or for a lump-sum payment.

children.label: b
children.text: The President may authorize leave of absence to a chief of mission excepted from this subchapter by for use in the United States and its territories or possessions. Leave so authorized does not constitute a leave system and may not be made the basis for a lump-sum payment.section 6301(2)(xii) of this title
accumulates for future use without regard to the limitation in ;section 6304(b) of this title

may not be made the basis for a lump-sum payment, except that civil service mariners of the Military Sealift Command on temporary promotion aboard ship may be paid the difference between their temporary and permanent rates of pay for leave accrued under this section and section 6303 and not otherwise used during the temporary promotion upon the expiration or termination of the temporary promotion; and
may not be made the basis for terminal leave except under such special or emergency circumstances as may be prescribed under the regulations of the Office.

children.label: d
children.text: With respect to an officer, crewmember, or other employee of the Department of Defense serving aboard an oceangoing vessel on an extended voyage, the first sentence in the matter preceding paragraph (1) of subsection (c) of this section shall be applied by substituting “7 calendar days” for “30 calendar days”.
